summary:
In pediatric populations, smallpox has become exceedingly rare due to widespread vaccinations; however, conditions like herpangina are more commonly encountered during specific seasons. Among the given choices, only smallpox fits the criteria for being rarely found in children nowadays. The prevalence rates make this distinction clear when comparing against typical childhood illnesses or less frequently occurring benign developmental anomalies. To reiterate succinctly:

Smallpox stands out as exceptionally uncommon compared to others listed because of its eradication through extensive immunizations worldwide. Other mentioned entities occur at varying frequencies within child demographics without reaching similar levels of scarcity. Thus,

\[ \text{\boxed{smallpox}} \] 

is identified correctly based upon updated medical literature emphasizing recent epidemiological trends post-vaccination campaigns.
